message.content:
You are mapping the KTP (календарно-тематический план) + ROADMAP subsystem of an educational platform at /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP.
Read fully and carefully:
- server/src/models/KtpCatalog.model.ts
- server/src/models/CanonicalRoadmap.model.ts
- server/src/services/ktp.service.ts
- server/src/services/roadmap.ai.service.ts
- server/src/roadmap/roadmap.rules.ts
- server/src/controllers/roadmap.controller.ts
And read server/src/services/roadmap.service.ts (it is ~1080 lines — read it in full across multiple reads if needed; this is the core file).
Goal: explain how the learning roadmap is constructed. Specifically:
- What is the KtpCatalog — a reference/справочник of КТП topics? What are its fields and granularity (subject? grade? topic list?)
- What is CanonicalRoadmap vs the live roadmap? Are roadmap NODES persisted entities or computed on the fly?
- How are roadmap nodes derived: from KTP catalog (live) or from book topics? (Memory hint: roadmap is built from the KTP reference live, not from linear book topics — verify or refute this against code.)
- What are roadmap NODES, EDGES, prerequisites/unlock rules? Where do they live?
- How does a roadmap node link back to content (topics/paragraphs/books) and to lessons and tests?
Return the structured map with exact field names and file:line. In evidence, quote the node/edge schema and the function that builds the roadmap.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- figma:figma-code-connect: Creates and maintains Figma Code Connect template files that map Figma components to code snippets. Use when the user mentions Code Connect, Figma component mapping, design-to-code translation, or asks to create/update .figma.ts or .figma.js files.
- figma:figma-create-new-file: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `create_new_file` tool call. NEVER call `create_new_file` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user wants a new blank Figma file — a new design, FigJam, or Slides file — or when you need a fresh file before calling `use_figma`. Usage — /figma-create-new-file [editorType] [fileName] (e.g. /figma-create-new-file figjam My Whiteboard, /figma-create-new-file slides Q3 Review)
- figma:figma-generate-design: Use this skill alongside figma-use when the task involves translating an application page, view, or multi-section layout into Figma. Triggers: 'write to Figma', 'create in Figma from code', 'push page to Figma', 'take this app/page and build it in Figma', 'create a screen', 'build a landing page in Figma', 'update the Figma screen to match code', 'convert this modal/dialog/drawer/panel to Figma'. This is the preferred workflow skill whenever the user wants to build or update a full page, modal, dialog, drawer, sidebar, panel, or any composed multi-section view in Figma from code or a description. Discovers design system components, variables, and styles from Code Connect files, existing screens, and library search, then imports them and assembles views incrementally section-by-section using design system tokens instead of hardcoded values.
- figma:figma-generate-diagram: MANDATORY prerequisite — load this skill BEFORE every `generate_diagram` tool call. NEVER call `generate_diagram` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user asks to create, generate, draw, render, sketch, or build a diagram — flowchart, architecture diagram, sequence diagram, ERD or entity-relationship diagram, state diagram or state machine, gantt chart, or timeline. Also trigger when the user mentions Mermaid syntax or wants a system architecture, decision tree, dependency graph, API call flow, auth handshake, schema, or pipeline visualized in FigJam. Routes to type-specific guidance, sets universal Mermaid constraints, and tells you when to use a different diagram type or skip the tool entirely (mindmaps, pie charts, class diagrams, etc.).
- figma:figma-generate-library: Build or update a professional-grade design system in Figma from a codebase. Use when the user wants to create variables/tokens, build component libraries, create individual components with proper variant sets and variable bindings, set up theming (light/dark modes), document foundations, or reconcile gaps between code and Figma. Also use when the user asks to create or generate any component in Figma — even a single one — since components require proper variable foundations, variant states, and design token bindings to be production-quality. This skill teaches WHAT to build and in WHAT ORDER — it complements the `figma-use` skill which teaches HOW to call the Plugin API. Both skills should be loaded together.
- figma:figma-swiftui: SwiftUI ↔ Figma translation. Use whenever the user mentions Swift, SwiftUI, iOS, iPhone, or iPad — in EITHER direction — translating a Figma design into SwiftUI (design → code), or pushing SwiftUI views / screens / tokens back into a Figma file (code → design). Triggers on phrases like 'implement this Figma design in SwiftUI', 'build this screen in Swift', 'push this SwiftUI view to Figma', 'mirror my Swift code in a Figma file', or whenever a Figma URL appears alongside `.swift` files / an `.xcodeproj`. Routes to a direction-specific reference doc; loads alongside `figma-use` for the code → design path.
- figma:figma-use: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `use_figma` tool call. NEVER call `use_figma` directly without loading this skill first. Skipping it causes common, hard-to-debug failures. Trigger whenever the user wants to perform a write action or a unique read action that requires JavaScript execution in the Figma file context — e.g. create/edit/delete nodes, set up variables or tokens, build components and variants, modify auto-layout or fills, bind variables to properties, or inspect file structure programmatically.
- figma:figma-use-figjam: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the FigJam context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- figma:figma-use-slides: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the Slides context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll analyze the KTP + ROADMAP subsystem by reading all the specified files.

content.content:
1	import mongoose, { Schema, Document, Model } from 'mongoose';
2	import { IKtpCatalog, IKtpTopic } from '../types/roadmap.types';
3	
4	/**
5	 * KTP CATALOG MODEL
6	 * Справочник КТП (календарно-тематическое планирование) по предмету.
7	 *
8	 * КТП — канонический упорядоченный список тем предмета (задаётся центром тестирования).
9	 * Роудмап строится ИЗ КТП: узел роудмапа = тема КТП, которая агрегирует материал
10	 * из тем книг (разных классов), замапленных на неё.
11	 *
12	 * _id встроенной темы КТП — стабильный, rename-safe идентификатор: на него ссылаются
13	 * Subject.Topic.ktpTopicIds[] и nodeId роудмапа (`ktp:{ktpTopicId}`).
14	 */
15	
16	const KtpTopicSchema = new Schema<IKtpTopic>(
17	  {
18	    title: { type: String, required: true, trim: true, minlength: 1, maxlength: 300 },
19	    description: { type: String, trim: true, maxlength: 8000 },
20	    order: { type: Number, required: true, min: 0 },
21	    /** Код темы от центра тестирования (для отображения/импорта; НЕ стабильный id) */
22	    code: { type: String, trim: true, maxlength: 80 },
23	    /** Явные пререквизиты (задел; по умолчанию используется линейный порядок по `order`) */
24	    prerequisiteKtpTopicIds: [{ type: Schema.Types.ObjectId }]
25	  },
26	  { _id: true, timestamps: false }
27	);
28	
29	type Doc = Document & Omit<IKtpCatalog, '_id'>;
30	
31	const KtpCatalogSchema = new Schema<Doc>(
32	  {
33	    subjectId: {
34	      type: Schema.Types.ObjectId,
35	      ref: 'Subject',
36	      required: true,
37	      index: true,
38	      unique: true
39	    },
40	    /** Год, на который актуален КТП (справочник ЦТ меняется ежегодно) */
41	    year: { type: Number, min: 2000, max: 2100 },
42	    version: { type: Number, required: true, default: 1, min: 1 },
43	    topics: { type: [KtpTopicSchema], required: true, default: [] }
44	  },
45	  { timestamps: true, collection: 'ktp_catalogs' }
46	);
47	
48	export const KtpCatalog: Model<Doc> = mongoose.model<Doc>('KtpCatalog', KtpCatalogSchema);
49

content.text: Now let me read the core file, roadmap.service.ts, in full.

I have the core file. The key live-build function `buildKtpCanonicalNodes` lives in `../utils/roadmapKtp.util`. Let me read that, plus the types file and the graph validator, to fully understand node derivation and link-back to content.
I now have a complete picture. Let me quickly check the helper utils `ktpNode.util` and `roadmapChapter.util` to confirm the nodeId/lessonId format and content extraction.
I now have a complete and verified picture of the KTP + ROADMAP subsystem. Let me compile the structured map.

input.subsystem: KTP (календарно-тематический план) + ROADMAP — learning roadmap construction

entities.name: KtpCatalog (КТП справочник)
fields: subjectId: ObjectId ref Subject — required, index, UNIQUE (one catalog per subject)
year?: Number (2000..2100) — год актуальности справочника ЦТ
relationships: each embedded topic._id is the stable, rename-safe id referenced by Subject.Topic.ktpTopicIds[] and by roadmap nodeId 'ktp:{topicId}'
entities.notes: This IS the reference/справочник of КТП topics. Granularity: per-subject, per-year, an ORDERED list of topics. NOT per-grade/class — grade info lives in book topics that map onto a КТП topic. The catalog is the canonical ordered topic list 'set by the testing center'.

entities.name: IKtpTopic (embedded КТП topic)
fields: _id: ObjectId — STABLE id (the rename-safe anchor; { _id: true })
order: Number — required, min 0 (defines linear sequence)
code?: String — max 80 (testing-center code, display/import only, NOT a stable id)
prerequisiteKtpTopicIds?: [ObjectId] — explicit prereqs (declared but UNUSED; default is linear order by `order`)
relationships: _id targeted by Subject chapter topic.ktpTopicIds[] (the mapping link from book topic -> КТП topic)
entities.notes: Granularity = single topic of a subject. prerequisiteKtpTopicIds is a placeholder ('задел') — buildKtpCanonicalNodes ignores it and uses linear order.

entities.name: CanonicalRoadmap
fields: subjectId: ObjectId ref Subject — required, index, UNIQUE
sourceMeta?: SourceMetaSchema (book/chapter provenance for AI-built maps)
relationships: FALLBACK store only: used when no КТП catalog exists. Created via admin AI gen (generateCanonicalFromBookAdmin) or manual JSON (upsertCanonicalAdmin)
entities.notes: NOT the primary roadmap source. resolveCanonical prefers a LIVE build from КТП and only falls back to this persisted doc. rebuildCanonicalFromKtp DELETES these docs (deleteMany) since the live build wins.

entities.name: ICanonicalRoadmapNode (the roadmap NODE)
fields: nodeId: String — required (for КТП build = 'ktp:{ktpTopicId}', see ktpNode.util.ts:9)
prerequisites: [String] — array of nodeIds (the EDGES; AND semantics)
metadata?: Mixed — holds subjectId, ktpTopicId, sources[], lessons[], primary bookId/chapterId/topicId, single `lesson`
relationships: prerequisites[] reference other nodeIds = the directed edges of the DAG
metadata.sources[] / metadata.lessons[].source link node -> book topic (bookId/chapterId/topicId) -> content
getCanonical/enrichPersonalNodeView resolve metadata.bookId+chapterId -> Test doc (latest by createdAt) to attach testId
entities.notes: For the LIVE КТП path these nodes are COMPUTED ON THE FLY (never persisted). For the fallback path they are persisted inside CanonicalRoadmap.nodes. Edges = prerequisites; unlock rule is in availabilityForNode.

entities.name: UserRoadmapProgress (personal/live progress)
fields: canonicalVersion: Number — required, min 1 (snapshot of bundle.version)
UNIQUE compound index { userId:1, subjectId:1 }; collection 'user_roadmap_progress'
relationships: merged against canonical nodes on every read via mergeProgressWithCanonical
entities.notes: This is the ONLY persisted per-user state. The 'personal roadmap' = canonical nodes (live) + this progress overlay (mastered/availability computed).

entities.name: IUserRoadmapNodeProgress (per-node progress)
fields: lowScoreFailCount?: Number — consecutive sub-80% fails (blocks retest at 3)
lessonReadAt?: Date — set when LAST lesson completed

entities.name: RoadmapAttempt
fields: userId, sessionId (unique per user), subjectId, nodeId, scorePercent, submittedAt
relationships: one row per submitted test session — used for idempotency in recordTestSubmitted (roadmap.service.ts:793-825)
entities.notes: Idempotency guard: duplicate sessionId (or 11000 dup-key) short-circuits and returns current snapshot.

entities.name: INodeLessonContent (consolidated lesson cache)
fields: subjectId, ktpTopicId, sourceHash (hash of mapped source texts), lessons[], generatedBy:'ai'|'manual'
relationships: caches AI-consolidated lessons for a КТП node keyed by ktpTopicId + sourceHash
entities.notes: sourceHash invalidates cache when mapped source texts change. Produced by roadmapAIService.consolidateLessonContent.

flows.name: Roadmap resolution (which source wins) — resolveCanonical
steps: ts:121 IF КТП exists AND topics.length>0 -> ts:122 buildKtpCanonicalNodes(subject, ktp) (LIVE BUILD)
ts:123-130 if build yields >=1 node -> return { version, nodes, fromChapters:true }. version = floor(max(subject.updatedAt, ktp.updatedAt)/1000) (time-derived, NOT a stored counter)
ts:134-136 if none -> 404 'Canonical roadmap not configured'
ts:138-145 return persisted nodes with fromChapters:false
flows.aiCalls: None. Pure DB read + in-memory graph build. AI is NOT used to resolve/order the live roadmap.

flows.name: LIVE node derivation from КТП — buildKtpCanonicalNodes (CONFIRMS memory hint)
steps: ts:47-65 build reverse index sourcesByKtp: walk Subject.books -> chapters (sorted by order) -> topics; for each topic.ktpTopicIds[k] push {bookId,chapterId,topicId,title}
ts:72-76 for each КТП topic: skip if 0 mapped sources (empty КТП topics are SKIPPED, chain not broken)
ts:80-92 build lessons[]: one lesson per mapped book topic (extractTopicText + makeChapterSummary), lessonId='{nodeId}::{topicId}'

ts:97-119 push node: title=КТП topic title, description=КТП topic desc||first lesson summary||title, prerequisites=[prevNodeId] (LINEAR chain), metadata={subjectId,ktpTopicId,sources[],lessons[],primary bookId/chapterId/topicId, single lesson}
flows.aiCalls: None in graph build. Lesson TEXT may later be AI-consolidated lazily (consolidateLessonContent) and cached in node_lesson_content; the node structure/ordering is deterministic.

flows.name: AI fallback canonical build (admin only) — generateCanonicalFromBookAdmin
steps: ai:59-84 builds prompt; ai:86-103 OpenAI chat (gpt-4o-mini, temp 0.4); ai:121-135 map+assertValidCanonicalNodes
ctrl:100-121 upsert CanonicalRoadmap with nextVersion, nodes, sourceMeta (book provenance)
flows.aiCalls: model 'gpt-4o-mini' (roadmap.ai.service.ts:7) builds the node graph+prerequisites from book text. Used ONLY for subjects without a КТП catalog (book-topic-derived path).

flows.name: Personal snapshot (nodes + progress overlay) — getPersonalSnapshot
steps: ts:296-317 load/upsert UserRoadmapProgress, re-merge if node count or version drifts (mergeProgressWithCanonical ts:174)
ts:330 availabilityForNode (ts:60): no prereqs -> 'available'; else 'available' iff ALL prereqs mastered, else 'locked' (the UNLOCK RULE)
ts:351-372 frontier = available & not-mastered; rank by compareFrontier (in-progress > unlocksCount > lowest bestScore); reasonForNode
flows.aiCalls: Optional, gated by ?ai=1 + OPENAI_API_KEY: roadmapAIService.enrichPersonalRoadmap (gpt-4o-mini) adds coachSummary/nextStepExplanation/nodeHints ONLY — never changes locked/available/mastered (deterministic).

flows.name: Test submission -> mastery update — recordTestSubmitted
steps: ts:833-839 bestScore=max(prev,score); mastered if isMasteredByBestScore (>=70%, roadmap.rules.ts:8); lowScoreFailCount += if <80% else reset
ts:853-863 persist UserRoadmapProgress; ts:865 recompute snapshot for delta

flows.name: Trial test fanout to КТП nodes — applyTrialKtpResults
steps: NB rows produced upstream by mapBookTopicToKtpNodeIds (roadmapKtp.util.ts:130) which maps a book topic -> 'ktp:*' node ids

flows.name: Admin rebuild from КТП — rebuildCanonicalFromKtp
steps: ts:1051-1054 require КТП catalog with topics
ts:1056-1061 buildKtpCanonicalNodes; error if no book topic mapped
ts:1066 CanonicalRoadmap.deleteMany (live build is authoritative, drop stale persisted snapshot)
ts:1069 bump Subject.updatedAt so learners' version recomputes on next read

input.reuse: Live КТП roadmap nodes are REGENERATED on the fly on every resolveCanonical call (no persisted node store for the КТП path). 'Caching' is effectively the КТП catalog + Subject.book-topic mappings themselves. Per-user progress (UserRoadmapProgress) is the only persisted overlay and is re-merged against freshly built nodes each read (mergeProgressWithCanonical), with canonicalVersion = floor(max(subject.updatedAt, ktp.updatedAt)/1000) acting as a change/version key. Roadmap version is time-derived, not a counter, so any edit to the subject or КТП bumps it. AI-consolidated lesson TEXT is cached in node_lesson_content keyed by (ktpTopicId, sourceHash) — invalidated when mapped source texts change. AI personal insights (coachSummary/hints) and the fallback CanonicalRoadmap doc are the only AI-generated content; the fallback doc is deleted whenever a КТП rebuild runs. testId per node is re-queried (latest Test by bookId+chapterId) on every getCanonical/enrichPersonalNodeView — not cached.
input.aiCost: AI cost is incurred at: (1) admin canonical generation per subject (generateCanonicalFromBookContent, one gpt-4o-mini call per admin rebuild — rare, shared across all users of that subject); (2) personal enrichment per request when ?ai=1 (enrichPersonalRoadmap, one gpt-4o-mini call PER USER PER getPersonal/getNext request — NOT cached, recomputed every call); (3) lazy lesson consolidation per КТП node (consolidateLessonContent, cached in node_lesson_content by sourceHash, so shared across users and regenerated only when sources change); (4) per-node lesson chat and lesson summary (chatLessonNode/generateLessonSummary, per user/per message; summary cached on canonical metadata.lesson.summary). The deterministic roadmap graph, mastery, unlock and recommendation logic cost NO AI.
problems: N+1 DB queries in getCanonical (roadmap.service.ts:194-229) and getPersonalSnapshot/enrichPersonalNodeView (ts:258-268, ts:398-403): a separate Test.findOne per node to resolve testId — scales O(nodes) queries per request; getHomeOverview (ts:588-624) and getPickerSubjects loop calls getPersonalSnapshot per subject, multiplying this.

Roadmap version is derived from floor(max(updatedAt)/1000) (ts:128, ts:1071-1074) — two edits within the same second collide on the same version; also couples version to wall-clock, making it non-monotonic if updatedAt is ever set backwards.

getPersonalSnapshot performs WRITES on a GET path (upsert/re-merge UserRoadmapProgress ts:298-317) — read endpoint has side effects and can race under concurrent requests; getHomeOverview/getPickerSubjects trigger these writes per subject.
Duplicated metadata-extraction + testId/chapterUrl logic exists twice: getCanonical (ts:196-227) and enrichPersonalNodeView (ts:251-281) — should be one helper.

Repeated boilerplate across recordTestSubmitted/markLessonRead/markLessonComplete/acknowledgeMaterialMastery/applyTrialKtpResults: same merge -> filter-out -> push -> mergeProgressWithCanonical -> findOneAndUpdate pattern (ts:759-770, 849-863, 895-909, 978-989, 1017-1031) — ripe for a single updateNodeProgress(nodeId, mutator) abstraction.

markLessonRead exists on both roadmapService (ts:878) and (per controller) roadmapLessonService — two lesson-read paths; node.lessonReadAt is set by both markLessonRead and markLessonComplete (last-lesson) which can conflict.

prerequisiteKtpTopicIds on IKtpTopic (KtpCatalog.model.ts:24) is declared but never read — buildKtpCanonicalNodes hardcodes a purely LINEAR prev->next chain (roadmapKtp.util.ts:101), so the DAG is always a path; the cycle-check (roadmapGraph.ts) and AND-prereq machinery are over-engineered for the live path.

KtpService.deleteTopic leaves a dangling reference (Subject.Topic.ktpTopicIds still points at the deleted КТП topic — explicit TODO 'Фаза 2' at ktp.service.ts:104), which would silently drop that book topic's source from the node.
Empty КТП topics are silently skipped (roadmapKtp.util.ts:76) — a curator mis-mapping yields a quietly shorter roadmap with no warning.

AI JSON parsing is brittle: parseJsonObject (roadmap.ai.service.ts:9-16) slices first '{' to last '}' and JSON.parse with minimal validation; nodeId from AI is free-form (no sanitization beyond String().trim()).
evidence: KtpCatalog schema (KtpCatalog.model.ts:31-43): subjectId unique+index, year, version, topics:[KtpTopicSchema]; embedded KtpTopicSchema (16-27): title, description, order(required,min0), code, prerequisiteKtpTopicIds:[ObjectId], { _id: true }
CanonicalNodeSchema (CanonicalRoadmap.model.ts:16-25): { nodeId:String required, title:String required, description, prerequisites:[String], metadata:Mixed }, { _id:false }

Live-vs-persisted decision (roadmap.service.ts:120-129): 'const ktp = await KtpCatalog.findOne({ subjectId }) ... if (ktp && ... ktp.topics.length > 0) { const ktpNodes = buildKtpCanonicalNodes(subject, ktp); if (ktpNodes.length > 0) { ... return { version, nodes: ktpNodes, fromChapters: true }; } }' then fallback CanonicalRoadmap.findOne (ts:133)

Node builder (roadmapKtp.util.ts:97-101): 'nodes.push({ nodeId, title: kt.title, description, prerequisites: prevNodeId ? [prevNodeId] : [], metadata: { subjectId: subjectIdStr, ktpTopicId: ktpTopicIdStr, sources, lessons, bookId: primary.bookId, chapterId: primary.chapterId, topicId: primary.topicId, lesson: {...} } }); prevNodeId = nodeId;'

Reverse index node<-book topic (roadmapKtp.util.ts:57-62): 'for (const k of topic.ktpTopicIds || []) { const key = String(k); const arr = sourcesByKtp.get(key) ?? []; arr.push({ bookId: bid, chapterId: cid, topicId: tid, title: topic.title }); sourcesByKtp.set(key, arr); }'

Unlock rule / EDGES (roadmap.service.ts:60-67): 'function availabilityForNode(node, mastered) { if (node.prerequisites.length === 0) return \'available\'; const allDone = node.prerequisites.every((id) => mastered.has(id)); return allDone ? \'available\' : \'locked\'; }'
nodeId/lessonId scheme (ktpNode.util.ts:9-22): ktpNodeId = 'ktp:'+ktpTopicId; ktpLessonId(nodeId, sourceTopicId) = nodeId+'::'+sourceTopicId — stable across rename/reorder (anchored to КТП topic _id)

Node->content/test link (roadmap.service.ts:203-215): 'if (bookId && chapterId) { const t = await Test.findOne({ subjectId: subjectOid, bookId, chapterId }).sort({ createdAt: -1 }).select(\'_id\').lean(); testId = t?._id?.toString(); } const chapterUrl = ... `/subjects/${subjectId}/books/${bookId}/chapters/${chapterId}${topicId ? `#topic-${topicId}` : ''}`'
Live build authoritative over persisted (roadmap.service.ts:1066): 'await CanonicalRoadmap.deleteMany({ subjectId: subjectOid });' in rebuildCanonicalFromKtp
Time-derived version (roadmap.service.ts:128): 'const version = Math.max(1, Math.floor(Math.max(sUpd, kUpd) / 1000));'

Memory hint CONFIRMED — header doc (roadmapKtp.util.ts:29-33): 'Строит canonical-узлы роудмапа ИЗ КТП. - Порядок узлов и линейные prerequisites берутся из порядка тем КТП (order). - Узел = тема КТП; агрегирует ВСЕ темы книг (разных классов), замапленные на неё (Subject.Topic.ktpTopicIds)'

content.text:
I've completed the analysis of the KTP + ROADMAP subsystem and returned the structured map. Here is a summary of the key findings:

**KtpCatalog is the справочник (reference)** of КТП topics — a per-subject (unique `subjectId`), per-`year`, ordered list of embedded topics (`server/src/models/KtpCatalog.model.ts:31-48`). Each embedded `IKtpTopic` has a stable `_id` (the rename-safe anchor), `title`, `description`, `order`, `code`, and an unused `prerequisiteKtpTopicIds`. Granularity is subject + ordered topic list — NOT grade/class (grade lives in the book topics that map onto a КТП topic).

**Memory hint CONFIRMED**: the live roadmap is built from the КТП reference, not from linear book topics. `resolveCanonical` (`roadmap.service.ts:115-146`) first checks `KtpCatalog`; if it has topics, it calls `buildKtpCanonicalNodes` (`roadmapKtp.util.ts:40-124`) which builds nodes ON THE FLY (never persisted). `CanonicalRoadmap` is only a fallback store used when no КТП catalog exists (admin AI-generated or manual JSON); a КТП rebuild even `deleteMany`s it (`roadmap.service.ts:1066`).

**Nodes/edges/unlock**: a node = one КТП topic, `nodeId = 'ktp:{ktpTopicId}'` (`ktpNode.util.ts:9`). Edges = `prerequisites[]` (array of nodeIds); the live build hardcodes a LINEAR `prev→next` chain from КТП `order`. Unlock rule in `availabilityForNode` (`roadmap.service.ts:60-67`): available iff no prereqs OR all prereqs mastered, else locked. Mastery thresholds: 70% normal / 80% trial (`roadmap.rules.ts:8-11`).

**Link-back to content/lessons/tests**: each node aggregates all mapped book topics (via `Subject.Topic.ktpTopicIds`) into `metadata.sources[]` and `metadata.lessons[]` (lessonId `'{nodeId}::{topicId}'`), plus a primary `bookId/chapterId/topicId`. `testId` is resolved by querying the latest `Test` for that book+chapter (`roadmap.service.ts:203-215`). Per-user state lives only in `UserRoadmapProgress` (`UserRoadmapProgress.model.ts`), re-merged against freshly built nodes each read.

Notable issues flagged: N+1 `Test.findOne` per node, time-derived (non-monotonic, second-collision) version, writes on GET paths, duplicated metadata/progress-update logic, and `deleteTopic` leaving dangling `ktpTopicIds` references (Phase-2 TODO at `ktp.service.ts:104`).
